79: - Escape Hatch Visibility Architecture

82:     **Core insight:** The architectural choice of dual-window Ghostty setup isn't just "nice to have" - it's a **required component** of escape-hatch spawning architecture.

104: - System Learning Loop
105:   - See: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/.kb/models/system-learning-loop.md
106:   - Summary:
107:     The System Learning Loop is the third layer of the Pressure Visibility System that automatically converts recurring context gaps into actionable improvements. It tracks gaps during spawns, identifies patterns using RecurrenceThreshold=3, and suggests specific actions (kn entries, beads issues, investigations). The system uses shell-aware command parsing to generate runnable commands with proper quoted string handling, and ensures minimum length requirements for downstream tools (kn requires 20+ chars). This creates a closed feedback loop: gaps → patterns → suggestions → improvements → fewer gaps.
108:     
109:     ---
110:   - Critical Invariants:
111:     1. **RecurrenceThreshold = 3** - Pattern detection balances noise (1) vs signal (3+)
112:     2. **All matching events must be marked resolved** - Not just the most recent one
113:     3. **FindRecurringGaps excludes resolved events** - Prevents resolved gaps from reappearing
114:     4. **Shell-aware command parsing required** - Quoted strings with spaces must be preserved
115:     5. **Minimum 20-character reasons** - kn decide/constrain requirement enforced at generation time
116:     6. **30-day retention window** - Gap events older than 30 days are pruned
117:     7. **Gap recording happens after gating** - Captures all gaps whether spawn proceeds or not
118:     
119:     ---
120:   - Why This Fails:
121:     ### Failure Mode 1: Resolved Gaps Keep Appearing
122:     
123:     **Symptom:** `orch learn` shows same gap after running `orch learn act` to resolve it
124:     
125:     **Root cause:** Two bugs combined:
126:     1. `RecordResolution` only marked the most recent event (used `break` after first match)
127:     2. `FindRecurringGaps` counted all events without filtering by Resolution field
128:     
129:     **Why it happens:**
130:     - Gap occurs 5 times → user resolves → only 1 event marked
131:     - Next `FindRecurringGaps` call counts 4 unresolved events → still above threshold (3)
132:     - Same pattern keeps appearing despite resolution
133:     
134:     **Impact:**
135:     - User frustration ("I already fixed this!")
136:     - Loss of trust in learning system
137:     - Duplicate work
138:     
139:     **Fix:**
140:     - `RecordResolution` now marks ALL matching events (removed `break`)
141:     - `FindRecurringGaps` filters out resolved events before counting
142:     
143:     **Source:** `.kb/investigations/2025-12-25-inv-orch-learn-resolved-gaps-still.md`
144:     
145:     ---
146:     
147:     ### Failure Mode 2: Generated Commands Fail Due to Broken Quoting
148:     
149:     **Symptom:** `orch learn act N` generates command that fails when executed
150:     
151:     **Root cause:** Using `strings.Fields()` to parse commands - splits on whitespace without respecting quotes
152:     
153:     **Why it happens:**
154:     - Command: `kn decide "auth" --reason "Used by: investigation. Occurred 5 times"`
155:     - `strings.Fields` splits into: `["kn", "decide", "\"auth\"", "--reason", "\"Used", "by:", "investigation.", ...]`
156:     - Shell receives mangled arguments, command fails
157:     
158:     **Impact:**
159:     - Learning loop broken - suggestions can't be executed
160:     - User must manually reconstruct commands
161:     - Reduces value of automated suggestions
162:     
163:     **Fix:**
164:     - Added `ParseShellCommand()` with shell-aware quote handling
165:     - Respects double and single quotes as argument delimiters
166:     - Added `ValidateCommand()` to catch malformed commands before execution
167:     
168:     **Source:** `.kb/investigations/2025-12-26-inv-orch-learn-act-commands-should.md`
169:     
170:     ---
171:     
172:     ### Failure Mode 3: Short Reasons Fail kn Validation
173:     
174:     **Symptom:** `orch learn act` generates kn command that fails with "reason too short" error
175:     
176:     **Root cause:** `generateReasonFromGaps` produced "Occurred N times" (16 chars) when gap events lacked skill/task metadata
177:     
178:     **Why it happens:**
179:     - Gap events with sparse metadata → only occurrence count available
180:     - "Occurred 3 times" = 16 characters
181:     - kn requires 20+ characters for `--reason` flag
182:     - Command fails validation at execution time
183:     
184:     **Impact:**
185:     - Generated commands unusable
186:     - User must manually edit reason strings
187:     - Learning loop broken for sparse gaps
188:

193: - Agent Lifecycle State Model
194:   - See: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/.kb/models/agent-lifecycle-state-model.md
195:   - Summary:
196:     Agent state exists across **four independent layers** (tmux windows, OpenCode in-memory, OpenCode on-disk, beads comments). These layers fall into two distinct categories: **state layers** (beads, workspace files) that represent what work was done, and **infrastructure layers** (OpenCode sessions, tmux windows) that represent transient execution resources. The dashboard reconciles these via a **Priority Cascade**: check beads issue status first (highest authority), then Phase comments, then registry state, then session existence. Status can appear "wrong" at the dashboard level while being "correct" at each individual layer - this is a measurement artifact from combining multiple sources of truth.
197:     
198:     ---
199:   - Critical Invariants:
200:     1. **Phase: Complete is agent's declaration** - Only agent can reach this, not orchestrator
201:     2. **Beads issue closed = canonical completion** - All status queries defer to beads
202:     3. **Session existence ≠ agent still working** - Sessions persist indefinitely
203:     4. **Status checks don't mutate state** - Calculation is read-only, no side effects
204:     5. **Multiple sources must be reconciled** - No single source has complete truth
205:     6. **Tmux windows are UI layer only** - Not authoritative for state
206:     
207:     ---
208:   - Why This Fails:
209:     ### Failure Mode 1: Dashboard Shows "Active" When Agent is Done
210:     
211:     **Symptom:** Dashboard shows agent as active, but `bd show <id>` says status=closed
212:     
213:     **Root cause:** Dashboard caching or SSE lag - hasn't received beads update yet
214:     
215:     **Why it happens:**
216:     - Agent reaches Phase: Complete
217:     - `orch complete` closes beads issue
218:     - Beads issue status = closed
219:     - Dashboard hasn't refreshed or polled beads yet
220:     - Dashboard still shows cached "active" state
221:     
222:     **Fix:** Refresh dashboard browser tab (forces beads query)
223:     
224:     **NOT the fix:** Deleting OpenCode session (treats symptom, not cause)
225:     
226:     ### Failure Mode 2: "Dead" Agents That Actually Completed
227:     
228:     **Symptom:** Dashboard shows "dead", but work is done and beads issue closed
229:     
230:     **Root cause:** Session cleanup happened before dashboard queried, cascade reached session check
231:     
232:     **Why it happens:**
233:     - Agent completed, beads issue closed
234:     - Session cleanup ran (manual or automatic)
235:     - Dashboard cascade: beads check → no issue (closed) → session check → no session → "dead"
236:     
237:     **Fix (Jan 8):** Priority Cascade puts beads/Phase check before session existence check
238:     
239:     ### Failure Mode 3: Agent Went Idle But Not Complete
240:     
241:     **Symptom:** Session status is "idle" but no `Phase: Complete` comment
242:     
243:     **Root cause:** Agent ran out of context, crashed, or didn't follow completion protocol
244:     
245:     **Why it happens:**
246:     - Session exhausts context (150k tokens)
247:     - Agent stops responding
248:     - SSE event: `session.status = idle`
249:     - No `Phase: Complete` was ever written
250:     - Dashboard shows "idle" or "waiting"
251:     
252:     **This is expected behavior.** Session idle ≠ work complete. Only agents that explicitly run `bd comment <id> "Phase: Complete"` are considered done.
253:     
254:     **Fix:** Check workspace for what agent accomplished, then either:
255:     - `orch complete <id> --force` if work is done
256:     - `orch abandon <id>` if work is incomplete
257:     
258:     ### Failure Mode 4: Cross-Project Agents Not Visible
259:     
260:     **Symptom:** Agent spawned with `--workdir /other/project` doesn't appear in dashboard
261:     
262:     **Root cause:** Dashboard only scans current project's `.orch/workspace/` directory
263:     
264:     **Why it happens:**
265:     - Workspace created in `/other/project/.orch/workspace/`
266:     - Dashboard running from `orch-go` only sees `orch-go/.orch/workspace/`
267:     - Cross-project discovery requires querying OpenCode sessions for unique directories
268:     
269:     **Fix (Jan 6):** Multi-project workspace cache built from OpenCode session directories
270:     
271:     ---

276: - Model Access and Spawn Paths
277:   - See: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/.kb/models/model-access-spawn-paths.md
278:   - Summary:
279:     Anthropic restricts Opus 4.5 access via fingerprinting that blocks API usage but allows Claude Code CLI with Max subscription. This constraint forced a **dual spawn architecture**: primary path (OpenCode API + Sonnet/Flash, headless, high concurrency) and escape hatch (Claude CLI + Opus, tmux, crash-resistant). The escape hatch exists because critical infrastructure work (fixing the spawn system itself) can't depend on what might fail. Model choice now encodes reliability requirements, not just quality preferences.
280:     
281:     ---
282:   - Critical Invariants:
283:     1. **Never spawn OpenCode infrastructure work without --backend claude --tmux**
284:        - Violation: Agent kills itself mid-execution when server restarts
285:     
286:     2. **Infrastructure detection runs before model auto-selection**
287:        - Priority 2.5 (between explicit flags and model-based selection)
288:        - Ensures auto-apply happens even without explicit --backend
289:     
290:     3. **Opus only accessible via Claude CLI backend**
291:        - API requests to Opus fail with auth error
292:        - Fingerprinting checks more than headers (TLS, HTTP/2 frames, ordering)
293:     
294:     4. **Escape hatch provides true independence**
295:        - Claude CLI binary ≠ OpenCode server
296:        - Tmux session persists across service restarts
297:        - Different authentication path (Max subscription OAuth)
298:     
299:     ---
300:   - Why This Fails:
301:     ### Failure Mode 1: Zombie Agents (Jan 8, 2026)
302:     
303:     **Symptom:** Agents tracked in registry but never actually ran
304:     
305:     **Root Cause:** Spawning with `--model opus` before understanding auth gate
306:     - orch created registry entry
307:     - OpenCode session created
308:     - Anthropic rejected API request (fingerprinting)
309:     - Agent hung in "running" state
310:     - Consumed concurrency slot without doing work
311:     
312:     **Examples:**
313:     - orch-go-mo0ja, orch-go-pzi2i, orch-go-aoei0, orch-go-gd1gd, orch-go-lwc3o
314:     
315:     **Fix:** Never use `--model opus` without `--backend claude`
316:     
317:     ### Failure Mode 2: Header Injection Conflicts (Jan 8, 2026)
318:     
319:     **Symptom:** Gemini Flash spawns hung after attempting Opus bypass
320:     
321:     **Root Cause:** Injected Claude Code headers (`x-app: cli`, `anthropic-version`, etc.) into OpenCode's Anthropic provider
322:     - Bypassed Opus gate (didn't work)
323:     - Broke Gemini spawns (headers conflicted with Bun fetch/SDK)
324:     - System-wide impact from localized change
325:     
326:     **Lesson:** Fingerprinting is more sophisticated than headers alone
327:     
328:     ### Failure Mode 3: Infrastructure Work Kills Itself
329:     
330:     **Symptom:** Agent fixing OpenCode server crashes mid-execution
331:     
332:     **Root Cause:** Agent spawned via OpenCode API, agent's fix restarts OpenCode server, agent's session killed
333:     
334:     **Solution:** Infrastructure work detection auto-applies `--backend claude --tmux`
335:     
336:     **Why auto-detection matters:**
337:     - Humans forget to add flags manually
338:     - Task description might not mention "opencode" explicitly
339:     - Keyword scan catches common patterns
340:     - Escape hatch becomes invisible safety net
341:

344: - Session Deletion Vectors
345:   - See: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/.kb/models/session-deletion-vectors.md
346:   - Summary:
347:     Active OpenCode sessions can become unfindable through **7 independent vectors** spanning 3 systems (disk-cleanup.sh, orch-go cleanup, OpenCode itself). The fundamental problem is that no "session is active, do not touch" lock exists, and multiple processes can delete sessions from the shared SQLite database without coordination. The Ctrl+D keybind is triple-bound (app exit, session delete, input delete), creating the highest-risk accidental deletion path. The disk-cleanup.sh vector was the first confirmed root cause (now fixed), but the bug persists because at least two other vectors remain open.
348:     
349:     ---
350:   - Critical Invariants:
351:     1. **Sessions exist in SQLite or they don't** - There is no "evicted but recoverable" state
352:     2. **NotFoundError = row deleted from DB** - Not a caching issue
353:     3. **Multiple processes share one SQLite DB** - No coordination protocol
354:     4. **Cascade deletes propagate silently** - Deleting a session kills all messages and parts
355:     5. **No "active session" lock exists** - Any process can delete any session at any time
356:     6. **JSON→SQLite migration is one-time** - Gate checks `opencode.db` existence, not whether sessions were imported. DB existed from Jan 27 schema migrations → 188 JSON sessions permanently orphaned, invisible to current code
357:     
358:     ---
359:   - Why This Fails:
360:     ### Failure Mode 1: Untracked Session Deletion (Vector #2)
361:     
362:     **Symptom:** Interactive TUI session disappears mid-conversation with NotFoundError
363:     
364:     **Root cause:** `cleanUntrackedDiskSessions()` in `clean_cmd.go:408-539` finds sessions not tracked by any `.orch/workspace/*/session_id` file, checks if they were updated in the last 5 minutes, and deletes them if idle.
365:     
366:     **Why TUI sessions are vulnerable:**
367:     - Interactive/orchestrator TUI sessions have NO workspace directory
368:     - No `.session_id` file means Layer 1 protection is bypassed entirely
369:     - If the user hasn't sent a message in >5 minutes (reading, thinking, context switch), Layer 2 (recency) marks it as orphaned
370:     - Layer 3 (`IsSessionProcessing()`) only runs for recently-active sessions
371:     - Session gets deleted via `client.DeleteSession(session.ID)` at line 525
372:     
373:     **Code path:**
374:     ```
375:     orch clean --sessions (or --all)
376:       → cleanUntrackedDiskSessions()
377:         → !trackedSessionIDs[session.ID]  ← TUI has no workspace, always true
378:         → now.Sub(updatedAt) > 5min       ← User paused, true
379:         → (skips IsSessionProcessing)     ← Only checked for recent sessions
380:         → client.DeleteSession(session.ID) ← SESSION DELETED
381:     ```
382:     
383:     **Fix needed:** Call `IsSessionProcessing()` for ALL untracked sessions, not just recently active ones. Cost: one API call per untracked session.
384:     
385:     ### Failure Mode 2: Accidental Ctrl+D Deletion (Vector #3)
386:     
387:     **Symptom:** Session vanishes after user presses Ctrl+D
388:     
389:     **Root cause:** Three keybinds share `ctrl+d`:
390:     - `app_exit: "ctrl+c,ctrl+d,<leader>q"` (config.ts:771)
391:     - `session_delete: "ctrl+d"` (config.ts:784)
392:     - `input_delete: "ctrl+d,delete,shift+delete"` (config.ts:878)
393:     
394:     **Why it happens:**
395:     1. User opens session list (`<leader>l`)
396:     2. User wants to exit the list, presses Ctrl+D (habit from terminal/vim)
397:     3. Session list dialog intercepts as `session_delete`, highlights session in red
398:     4. If user presses Ctrl+D again (common stutter or habit), session is permanently deleted
399:     5. TUI crashes with NotFoundError on next render cycle
400:     
401:     The confirmation ("Press ctrl+d again to confirm") is displayed as red-highlighted title text that may not be noticed in a fast interaction.
402:     
403:     **Fix needed:** Rebind `session_delete` to a non-conflicting key, or add a modal confirmation dialog.
404:     
405:     ### Failure Mode 3: External Process Deletion (Vector #4)
406:     
407:     **Symptom:** Session disappears without user action
408:     
409:     **Root cause:** `DELETE /session/:id` route has no authentication and no coordination. Any local process can delete

417: - Beads Integration Architecture
418:   - See: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/.kb/models/beads-integration-architecture.md
419:   - Summary:
420:     Beads integration uses **RPC-first with CLI fallback** pattern: try native Go RPC client (fast, no process spawn), fall back to CLI subprocess if daemon unavailable. The integration operates at **three points in agent lifecycle**: spawn (create issue), work (report phase via comments), complete (close with reason). **Auto-tracking** creates issues automatically unless `--no-track` flag set. The RPC client lives in **pkg/beads** (never shell out with `exec.Command` directly) and provides 10x performance improvement over CLI (single RPC call vs subprocess spawn + JSON parse).
421:     
422:     ---
423:   - Why This Fails:
424:     ### 1. RPC Client Unavailable
425:     
426:     **What happens:** RPC calls fail, client falls back to CLI, performance degrades.
427:     
428:     **Root cause:** Beads daemon not running. RPC socket `~/.beads/daemon.sock` doesn't exist.
429:     
430:     **Why detection is hard:** Fallback is silent. No warning that RPC failed. User sees slow performance, doesn't know why.
431:     
432:     **Fix:** Start beads daemon: `bd daemon start` or ensure launchd starts it on boot.
433:     
434:     **Detection:** Log RPC failures, surface in `orch doctor` health check.
435:     
436:     ### 2. Beads ID Not Found
437:     
438:     **What happens:** `orch complete orch-go-abc1` fails with "issue not found".
439:     
440:     **Root cause:** Cross-project spawn. Issue created in orch-knowledge, but trying to complete from orch-go. Beads scoped to current directory's `.beads/`.
441:     
442:     **Why detection is hard:** Beads ID looks valid (correct format), but doesn't exist in current project's `.beads/issues.jsonl`.
443:     
444:     **Fix:** `cd` into correct project before completion, or use `--workdir` flag.
445:     
446:     **Prevention:** `orch complete` should detect project from workspace, auto-cd.
447:     
448:     ### 3. Auto-Tracking Creates Duplicates
449:     
450:     **What happens:** `orch spawn` creates issue, but issue already exists for same work.
451:     
452:     **Root cause:** User creates issue manually, then spawns with auto-tracking. Both create issue.
453:     
454:     **Why detection is hard:** No deduplication. Beads doesn't check if similar issue exists.
455:

469: - Dashboard Architecture
470:   - See: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/.kb/models/dashboard-architecture.md
471:   - Summary:
472:     The Swarm Dashboard is a Svelte 5 web UI served by `orch serve` (Go backend) that provides real-time monitoring of agent status, daemon health, and operational metrics.
473:     
474:     **Critical context (Option A+):** The dashboard is Dylan's (meta-orchestrator's) ONLY observability layer. He does not use CLI tools directly. Dashboard failure = Dylan is blind. This makes dashboard reliability tier-0 infrastructure. See orchestrator skill "Observability Architecture (Option A+)" section.
475:     
476:     The architecture uses a **two-mode design** (Operational/Historical) to separate daily coordination from deep analysis. SSE connections enable real-time updates but are constrained by HTTP/1.1's 6-connection limit. Progressive disclosure and stable sorting prevent information overload while maintaining scan-ability.
477:     
478:     ---
479:   - Critical Invariants:
480:     1. **Two-mode design is mutually exclusive** - Cannot show both Operational and Historical views simultaneously
481:     2. **SSE Events auto-connect, Agentlog is opt-in** - Connection pool management
482:     3. **beadsFetchThreshold controls remote queries** - 5+ ready issues triggers `bd ready` shell-out
483:     4. **Progressive disclosure via collapsed panels** - Event panels start collapsed, expand on click
484:     5. **Stable sort maintains scan-ability** - Agent order doesn't change unless status changes
485:     6. **Early filtering reduces payload size** - Backend filters before sending to frontend
486:     
487:     ---
488:   - Why This Fails:
489:     ### Failure Mode 1: Connection Pool Exhaustion
490:     
491:     **Symptom:** API fetches hang or timeout when SSE panels open
492:     
493:     **Root cause:** HTTP/1.1 allows only 6 connections per origin; SSE occupies slots
494:     
495:     **Why it happens:**
496:     - Events SSE (auto-connect): 1 slot
497:     - Agentlog SSE (auto-connect before fix): 1 slot
498:     - Remaining 4 slots for API fetches
499:     - If 5+ API requests concurrent, some block
500:     
501:     **Fix (Jan 5):** Made Agentlog SSE opt-in via Follow button, freeing 1 slot
502:     
503:     ### Failure Mode 2: Slow Dashboard Load with 100+ Agents
504:     
505:     **Symptom:** Dashboard takes 5-10 seconds to load with many agents
506:     
507:     **Root cause:** `/api/agents` endpoint performs expensive operations (OpenCode queries, beads parsing) synchronously
508:     
509:     **Why it happens:**
510:     - Each agent requires OpenCode session query
511:     - Full beads issue parsing for each agent
512:     - No caching, recomputed on every request
513:     
514:     **Fix (Jan 6):** Response caching with 2-second TTL, reduced load time to <1 second
515:     
516:     ### Failure Mode 3: Information Overload in Operational Mode
517:     
518:     **Symptom:** Users overwhelmed by full swarm map with 50+ agents
519:     
520:     **Root cause:** Single view tried to serve both daily coordination and deep analysis
521:     
522:     **Why it happens:**
523:     - Operational needs: "What's ready? What's broken?"
524:     - Historical needs: "Show me everything, all filters, full archive"
525:     - One view can't optimize for both
526:     
527:     **Fix (Jan 7):** Two-mode design - Operational (focused) vs Historical (comprehensive)
528:     
529:     ### Failure Mode 4: Plugin Cascade (Dashboard "Disconnected" Despite Services Running)
530:     
531:     **Symptom:** Dashboard shows "disconnected", `overmind status` shows all 3 services running, but `orch status` returns HTTP 500
532:     
533:     **Root cause:** OpenCode plugin error (e.g., v1→v2 API incompatibility) crashes OpenCode's internal request handling
534:     
535:     **Why it happens:**
536:     - OpenCode loads plugins at startup
537:     - Bad plugin throws error on every request
538:     - `/api/agents` calls OpenCode → gets 500
539:     - Dashboard can't fetch agent data → shows "disconnected"
540:     - overmind sees process running (not crashed) → reports "running"
541:     
542:     **Cascade:**
543:     ```
544:     Plugin error → OpenCode internal 500 → orch status fails → API can't get agents → Dashboard "disconnected"
545:     ```
546:     
547:     **Fix (Jan 14):** Disable plugins, restart OpenCode, re-enable one-by-one. Root cause was session-resume.js using v1 API (object export) instead of v2 (function export).
548:     
549:     **Key insight:** Dashboard can appear "down" while all processes are technically "running". Health checks must verify data flow, not just port availability.

1395: ### Implementation Phase (Verification-First Mode)

1407: **Key difference from TDD:**

81: 	Long: `Start a new orchestrator work session with a focus goal.
82: 
83: The session tracks:
84: - Your focus goal
85: - When the session started
86: - Agents spawned during the session
87: 
88: If a session is already active, it will be replaced.

90: Examples:

100: func runSessionStart(goal string) error {
101: 	store, err := session.New("")
102: 	if err != nil {
103: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to load session: %w", err)
104: 	}
105: 
106: 	// Check if session was already active
107: 	wasActive := store.IsActive()
108: 
109: 	// Get current working directory (project directory)
110: 	projectDir, err := os.Getwd()
111: 	if err != nil {
112: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get project directory: %w", err)
113: 	}
114: 
115: 	// Generate session name in format {project}-{count}
116: 	sessionName, err := session.GenerateSessionName(projectDir)
117: 	if err != nil {
118: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to generate session name: %v\n", err)
119: 		// Fall back to timestamp-based name
120: 		sessionName = fmt.Sprintf("session-%s", time.Now().Format("20060102-1504"))
121: 	}
122: 
123: 	// Rename tmux window to match session name (auto-naming pattern)
124: 	if err := tmux.RenameCurrentWindow(sessionName); err != nil {
125: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to rename tmux window: %v\n", err)
126: 		// Continue anyway - window renaming is nice-to-have
127: 	}
128: 
129: 	// Capture the window name AFTER renaming - this is the name used for session directories
130: 	// We store this in the session so that session end can archive to the correct directory
131: 	// even if called from a different tmux window
132: 	windowName, err := tmux.GetCurrentWindowName()
133: 	if err != nil {
134: 		// Fall back to session name if we can't get window name
135: 		windowName = sessionName
136: 	}
137: 
138: 	// Create active session handoff in project-specific location
139: 	// This replaces the global ~/.orch workspace with project/.orch/session/{sessionName}/active/
140: 	handoffPath, err := createActiveSessionHandoff(goal, sessionName)
141: 	if err != nil {
142: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to create active session handoff: %v\n", err)
143: 		// Continue anyway - handoff is nice-to-have for interactive sessions
144: 	}
145: 
146: 	// Derive workspace path from handoff path (handoff is in the workspace directory)
147: 	// If handoff creation failed, workspace path will be empty string
148: 	workspacePath := ""
149: 	if handoffPath != "" {
150: 		workspacePath = filepath.Dir(handoffPath)
151: 	}
152: 
153: 	// Start the session with the captured window name and workspace path
154: 	if err := store.Start(goal, windowName, workspacePath); err != nil {
155: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to start session: %w", err)
156: 	}
157: 
158: 	// Progressive Session Capture: Prompt for TLDR and Where We Started
159: 	// Part of decision 2026-01-14 - capture context when it's freshest
160: 	if handoffPath != "" {
161: 		responses, err := promptForStartSections()
162: 		if err != nil {
163: 			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to collect start section responses: %v\n", err)
164: 			// Continue anyway - we can still end the session and prompt for these later
165: 		} else if len(responses) > 0 {
166: 			if err := updateHandoffWithStartResponses(handoffPath, responses); err != nil {
167: 				fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to update handoff with responses: %v\n", err)
168: 			} else {
169: 				fmt.Println("   ✅ Initial context captured in SESSION_HANDOFF.md")
170: 			}
171: 		}
172: 	}
173: 
174: 	// Log the session start
175: 	logger := events.NewLogger(events.DefaultLogPath())
176: 	eventData := map[string]interface{}{
177: 		"goal":         goal,
178: 		"was_active":   wasActive,
179: 		"started_at":   time.Now().Format(session.TimeFormat),
180: 		"session_name": sessionName,
181: 	}
182: 	if handoffPath != "" {
183: 		eventData["handoff_path"] = handoffPath
184: 	}
185: 	event := events.Event{
186: 		Type:      "session.started",
187: 		Timestamp: time.Now().Unix(),
188: 		Data:      eventData,
189: 	}
190: 	if err := logger.Log(event); err != nil {
191: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to log event: %v\n", err)
192: 	}
193: 
194: 	if wasActive {
195: 		fmt.Println("Previous session ended.")
196: 	}
197: 	fmt.Printf("Session started: %s\n", goal)
198: 	fmt.Printf("  Name:       %s\n", sessionName)
199: 	fmt.Printf("  Start time: %s\n", time.Now().Format("15:04"))
200: 	if handoffPath != "" {
201: 		fmt.Printf("  Handoff:    %s\n", handoffPath)
202: 	}

204: 	// Surface reflection suggestions for high-count synthesis opportunities

206: 	surfaceReflectSuggestions()
207: 
208: 	// Surface focus guidance - group ready issues into thematic threads
209: 	// Part of Capture at Context principle - surface context when it matters
210: 	surfaceFocusGuidance()
211: 
212: 	return nil
213: }

215: // SynthesisWarningThreshold is the minimum count of investigations to show a warning.
216: // Matches SynthesisIssueThreshold in kb-cli to maintain consistency.
217: const SynthesisWarningThreshold = 10

223: // InvestigationPromotionThreshold is the count above which session end will warn.
224: // Gates accumulation of promotion candidates that need triage.
225: const InvestigationPromotionThreshold = 5

227: // surfaceFocusGuidance loads ready issues and displays them grouped into thematic threads.
228: // This helps orchestrators orient at session start: "Here are your active threads. What's nagging you?"
229: // Part of Capture at Context principle.
230: func surfaceFocusGuidance() {
231: 	guidance, err := focus.GenerateFocusGuidance()
232: 	if err != nil {
233: 		// Failed to load issues - silently skip (not critical)
234: 		return
235: 	}
236: 
237: 	if guidance.TotalIssues == 0 {
238: 		// No ready issues - brief message only
239: 		fmt.Println("\n📋 No ready issues found")
240: 		return
241: 	}
242: 
243: 	// Display formatted guidance
244: 	fmt.Print(focus.FormatFocusGuidance(guidance))
245: }
246: 
247: // surfaceReflectSuggestions loads and displays synthesis warnings from reflect-suggestions.json.
248: // This proactively surfaces consolidation needs at session start so orchestrators are aware
249: // of accumulated investigation clusters that need synthesis into guides.
250: func surfaceReflectSuggestions() {
251: 	suggestions, err := daemon.LoadSuggestions()
252: 	if err != nil || suggestions == nil {
253: 		// No suggestions file or failed to load - silently skip
254: 		return
255: 	}
256: 
257: 	// Check freshness - skip stale suggestions
258: 	if time.Since(suggestions.Timestamp).Hours() > SuggestionFreshnessHours {
259: 		return
260: 	}
261: 
262: 	// Filter to high-count synthesis opportunities
263: 	var highCount []daemon.SynthesisSuggestion
264: 	for _, s := range suggestions.Synthesis {
265: 		if s.Count >= SynthesisWarningThreshold {
266: 			highCount = append(highCount, s)
267: 		}
268: 	}
269: 
270: 	if len(highCount) == 0 {
271: 		return
272: 	}
273: 
274: 	// Display synthesis warnings
275: 	fmt.Println()
276: 	fmt.Println("📚 SYNTHESIS OPPORTUNITIES")
277: 	fmt.Printf("   %d topics have accumulated %d+ investigations:\n", len(highCount), SynthesisWarningThreshold)
278: 
279: 	// Show top 5 topics
280: 	maxShow := 5
281: 	if len(highCount) < maxShow {
282: 		maxShow = len(highCount)
283: 	}
284: 	for i := 0; i < maxShow; i++ {
285: 		s := highCount[i]
286: 		fmt.Printf("   • %s: %d investigations → kb create guide \"%s\"\n", s.Topic, s.Count, s.Topic)
287: 	}
288: 	if len(highCount) > maxShow {
289: 		fmt.Printf("   ... and %d more topics\n", len(highCount)-maxShow)
290: 	}
291: 	fmt.Printf("   Run 'kb reflect --type synthesis' for details.\n")
292: }

307: // checkInvestigationPromotions runs kb reflect --type investigation-promotion --format json
308: // and returns the count of promotion candidates. Returns 0 and logs warning on error.
309: func checkInvestigationPromotions() int {
310: 	cmd := exec.Command("kb", "reflect", "--type", "investigation-promotion", "--format", "json")
311: 	output, err := cmd.Output()
312: 	if err != nil {
313: 		// kb reflect may not be available or may fail - not critical, just skip
314: 		return 0
315: 	}
316: 
317: 	var result InvestigationPromotionResult
318: 	if err := json.Unmarshal(output, &result); err != nil {
319: 		// Parse error - skip silently
320: 		return 0
321: 	}
322: 
323: 	return len(result.InvestigationPromotion)
324: }

326: // gateInvestigationPromotions checks for accumulated investigation promotion candidates
327: // and prompts user to triage if above threshold. Returns error if user aborts.
328: // This is a gate at session end to prevent accumulation of promotion candidates.
329: func gateInvestigationPromotions() error {
330: 	count := checkInvestigationPromotions()
331: 	if count <= InvestigationPromotionThreshold {
332: 		return nil // Below threshold, proceed
333: 	}
334: 
335: 	fmt.Println()
336: 	fmt.Printf("⚠️  INVESTIGATION PROMOTION BACKLOG\n")
337: 	fmt.Printf("   %d investigations need promotion review (threshold: %d)\n", count, InvestigationPromotionThreshold)
338: 	fmt.Printf("   Run 'kb reflect --type investigation-promotion' to triage.\n")
339: 	fmt.Println()
340: 
341: 	// Prompt user to confirm proceeding
342: 	reader := bufio.NewReader(os.Stdin)
343: 	fmt.Print("   Continue ending session anyway? (y/N): ")
344: 	response, err := reader.ReadString('\n')
345: 	if err != nil {
346: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to read input: %w", err)
347: 	}
348: 	response = strings.TrimSpace(strings.ToLower(response))
349: 
350: 	if response != "y" && response != "yes" {
351: 		fmt.Println("   Session end aborted. Please triage investigation promotions first.")
352: 		return fmt.Errorf("session end aborted: investigation promotion backlog needs triage")
353: 	}
354: 
355: 	return nil
356: }

407: func runSessionStatus() error {
408: 	store, err := session.New("")
409: 	if err != nil {
410: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to load session: %w", err)
411: 	}
412: 
413: 	sess := store.Get()
414: 
415: 	// Build output
416: 	output := SessionStatusOutput{
417: 		Active: sess != nil,
418: 	}
419: 
420: 	if sess != nil {
421: 		output.Goal = sess.Goal
422: 		output.StartedAt = sess.StartedAt.Format(session.TimeFormat)
423: 		output.Duration = formatSessionDuration(store.Duration())
424: 		output.WorkspacePath = sess.WorkspacePath
425: 
426: 		// Get spawn statuses with reconciliation
427: 		statuses := store.GetSpawnStatuses(serverURL)
428: 		output.Spawns = statuses
429: 
430: 		// Count by state
431: 		counts := &SpawnCounts{Total: len(statuses)}
432: 		for _, s := range statuses {
433: 			switch s.State {
434: 			case "active":
435: 				counts.Active++
436: 			case "completed":
437: 				counts.Completed++
438: 			case "phantom":
439: 				counts.Phantom++
440: 			}
441: 		}
442: 		output.Counts = counts
443: 
444: 		// Get checkpoint status using orchestrator thresholds
445: 		// (orch session is for orchestrator sessions, not agent sessions)
446: 		output.Checkpoint = store.GetCheckpointStatusWithType(session.SessionTypeOrchestrator)
447: 	}
448: 
449: 	// JSON output
450: 	if sessionJSON {
451: 		data, err := json.MarshalIndent(output, "", "  ")
452: 		if err != nil {
453: 			return fmt.Errorf("failed to marshal JSON: %w", err)
454: 		}
455: 		fmt.Println(string(data))
456: 		return nil
457: 	}
458: 
459: 	// Human-readable output
460: 	if !output.Active {
461: 		fmt.Println("No active session")
462: 		fmt.Println("\nStart a session with: orch session start \"your goal\"")
463: 		return nil
464: 	}
465: 
466: 	fmt.Printf("Session active:\n")
467: 	fmt.Printf("  Goal:      %s\n", output.Goal)
468: 	fmt.Printf("  Duration:  %s", output.Duration)
469: 
470: 	// Show checkpoint status inline with duration
471: 	if output.Checkpoint != nil {
472: 		switch output.Checkpoint.Level {
473: 		case "exceeded":
474: 			fmt.Printf(" ⛔")
475: 		case "strong":
476: 			fmt.Printf(" 🔴")
477: 		case "warning":
478: 			fmt.Printf(" 🟡")
479: 		}
480: 	}
481: 	fmt.Println()
482: 
483: 	if output.WorkspacePath != "" {
484: 		fmt.Printf("  Workspace: %s\n", output.WorkspacePath)
485: 	}
486: 
487: 	fmt.Printf("  Spawns:    %d total", output.Counts.Total)
488: 	if output.Counts.Active > 0 {
489: 		fmt.Printf(" (%d active", output.Counts.Active)
490: 		if output.Counts.Completed > 0 {
491: 			fmt.Printf(", %d completed", output.Counts.Completed)
492: 		}
493: 		if output.Counts.Phantom > 0 {
494: 			fmt.Printf(", %d phantom", output.Counts.Phantom)
495: 		}
496: 		fmt.Printf(")")
497: 	}
498: 	fmt.Println()
499: 
500: 	// Show spawn details if any
501: 	if len(output.Spawns) > 0 {
502: 		fmt.Println("\nSpawns:")
503: 		for _, spawn := range output.Spawns {
504: 			stateIcon := stateToIcon(spawn.State)
505: 			age := formatSessionDuration(time.Since(spawn.SpawnedAt))
506: 			fmt.Printf("  %s %s (%s) - %s ago\n", stateIcon, spawn.BeadsID, spawn.Skill, age)
507: 		}
508: 	}
509: 
510: 	// Show checkpoint warning if applicable
511: 	if output.Checkpoint != nil && output.Checkpoint.Level != "ok" {
512: 		fmt.Println()
513: 		switch output.Checkpoint.Level {
514: 		case "exceeded":
515: 			fmt.Printf("⛔ CHECKPOINT EXCEEDED: %s\n", output.Checkpoint.Message)
516: 			fmt.Println("   Session has run too long. Quality may be degraded.")
517: 			fmt.Println("   Action: Run 'orch session end' and start fresh.")
518: 		case "strong":
519: 			fmt.Printf("🔴 CHECKPOINT STRONGLY RECOMMENDED: %s\n", output.Checkpoint.Message)
520: 			fmt.Printf("   Time until max: %s\n", formatSessionDuration(output.Checkpoint.NextThreshold))
521: 			fmt.Println("   Action: Write SESSION_HANDOFF.md, consider ending session.")
522: 		case "warning":
523: 			fmt.Printf("🟡 CHECKPOINT SUGGESTED: %s\n", output.Checkpoint.Message)
524: 			fmt.Printf("   Time until strong warning: %s\n", formatSessionDuration(output.Checkpoint.NextThreshold))
525: 			fmt.Println("   Action: Assess progress, write interim handoff if needed.")
526: 		}
527: 	}
528: 
529: 	return nil
530: }

532: // stateToIcon converts state to a visual indicator.
533: func stateToIcon(state string) string {
534: 	switch state {
535: 	case "active":
536: 		return "🟢"
537: 	case "completed":
538: 		return "✅"
539: 	case "phantom":
540: 		return "👻"
541: 	default:
542: 		return "❓"
543: 	}
544: }

546: // formatSessionDuration formats a duration in a human-readable way for session display.
547: func formatSessionDuration(d time.Duration) string {
548: 	if d < time.Minute {
549: 		return fmt.Sprintf("%ds", int(d.Seconds()))
550: 	}
551: 	if d < time.Hour {
552: 		return fmt.Sprintf("%dm", int(d.Minutes()))
553: 	}
554: 	hours := int(d.Hours())
555: 	mins := int(d.Minutes()) % 60
556: 	if mins == 0 {
557: 		return fmt.Sprintf("%dh", hours)
558: 	}
559: 	return fmt.Sprintf("%dh%dm", hours, mins)
560: }

585: func runSessionEnd() error {
586: 	store, err := session.New("")
587: 	if err != nil {
588: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to load session: %w", err)
589: 	}
590: 
591: 	if !store.IsActive() {
592: 		fmt.Println("No active session to end")
593: 		return nil
594: 	}
595: 
596: 	// Gate: Check for accumulated investigation promotion candidates
597: 	// This prevents backlog accumulation by prompting triage before session end
598: 	if err := gateInvestigationPromotions(); err != nil {
599: 		return err
600: 	}
601: 
602: 	// Get session info before ending - IMPORTANT: Get the session object to access WindowName
603: 	// which was captured at session start. This is used for archiving, NOT GetCurrentWindowName().
604: 	sess := store.Get()
605: 	duration := store.Duration()
606: 	spawnCount := store.SpawnCount()
607: 
608: 	// Get spawn statuses for final summary
609: 	statuses := store.GetSpawnStatuses(serverURL)
610: 	activeCount := 0
611: 	for _, s := range statuses {
612: 		if s.State == "active" {
613: 			activeCount++
614: 		}
615: 	}
616: 
617: 	// Get project directory
618: 	projectDir, err := os.Getwd()
619: 	if err != nil {
620: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to get project directory: %v\n", err)
621: 	} else {
622: 		// Use the stored window name from session start, NOT GetCurrentWindowName()
623: 		// This ensures we archive to the correct directory even if called from a different window
624: 		windowName := sess.WindowName
625: 		if windowName == "" {
626: 			// Fallback for sessions created before WindowName was added
627: 			windowName, err = tmux.GetCurrentWindowName()
628: 			if err != nil {
629: 				fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to get window name: %v\n", err)
630: 				windowName = "default"
631: 			}
632: 		}
633: 
634: 		// Complete and archive the session handoff
635: 		// This validates unfilled sections, prompts for completion, then archives
636: 		if err := completeAndArchiveHandoff(projectDir, windowName); err != nil {
637: 			// Only warn - not all sessions will have active handoffs (pre-active-pattern sessions)
638: 			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to complete/archive session handoff: %v\n", err)
639: 		}
640: 	}
641: 
642: 	// End the session
643: 	ended, err := store.End()
644: 	if err != nil {
645: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to end session: %w", err)
646: 	}
647: 
648: 	// Log the session end
649: 	logger := events.NewLogger(events.DefaultLogPath())
650: 	event := events.Event{
651: 		Type:      "session.ended",
652: 		Timestamp: time.Now().Unix(),
653: 		Data: map[string]interface{}{
654: 			"goal":          ended.Goal,
655: 			"started_at":    ended.StartedAt.Format(session.TimeFormat),
656: 			"duration":      duration.String(),
657: 			"spawn_count":   spawnCount,
658: 			"active_at_end": activeCount,
659: 		},
660: 	}
661: 	if err := logger.Log(event); err != nil {
662: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to log event: %v\n", err)
663: 	}
664: 
665: 	fmt.Printf("Session ended: %s\n", ended.Goal)
666: 	fmt.Printf("  Duration:  %s\n", formatSessionDuration(duration))
667: 	fmt.Printf("  Spawns:    %d total\n", spawnCount)
668: 
669: 	if activeCount > 0 {
670: 		fmt.Printf("\n⚠️  %d agent(s) still active. Use 'orch status' to monitor.\n", activeCount)
671: 	}
672: 
673: 	// Show checkpoint advice based on session duration using orchestrator thresholds
674: 	orchThresholds := session.DefaultOrchestratorThresholds()
675: 	if duration >= orchThresholds.Max {
676: 		fmt.Printf("\n⛔ Session exceeded %s checkpoint max.\n", formatSessionDuration(orchThresholds.Max))
677: 		fmt.Println("   Consider shorter sessions to maintain quality.")
678: 	} else if duration >= orchThresholds.Strong {
679: 		fmt.Printf("\n🟡 Session was %s+. Good to hand off, but review quality of late work.\n", formatSessionDuration(orchThresholds.Strong))
680: 	}
681: 
682: 	return nil
683: }

760: 
761: After window-scoping, they're stored in:

774: func runSessionMigrate() error {
775: 	// Get current directory to find .orch/session
776: 	currentDir, err := os.Getwd()
777: 	if err != nil {
778: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get current directory: %w", err)
779: 	}
780: 
781: 	// Find project root by walking up to .orch directory
782: 	projectDir := currentDir
783: 	for {
784: 		sessionDir := filepath.Join(projectDir, ".orch", "session")
785: 		if _, err := os.Stat(sessionDir); err == nil {
786: 			break
787: 		}
788: 		parent := filepath.Dir(projectDir)
789: 		if parent == projectDir {
790: 			return fmt.Errorf("no .orch/session directory found (not in an orch-managed project)")
791: 		}
792: 		projectDir = parent
793: 	}
794: 
795: 	sessionBaseDir := filepath.Join(projectDir, ".orch", "session")
796: 
797: 	// Get current window name for migration target
798: 	windowName, err := tmux.GetCurrentWindowName()
799: 	if err != nil {
800: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get window name: %w", err)
801: 	}
802: 
803: 	// Check for legacy handoffs (non-window-scoped directories)
804: 	entries, err := os.ReadDir(sessionBaseDir)
805: 	if err != nil {
806: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to read session directory: %w", err)
807: 	}
808: 
809: 	var legacyDirs []string
810: 	for _, entry := range entries {
811: 		if !entry.IsDir() {
812: 			continue
813: 		}
814: 		// Legacy directories are timestamp format: YYYY-MM-DD-HHMM
815: 		// Window-scoped directories are names (e.g., "default", "pw", "og-feat-...")
816: 		name := entry.Name()
817: 		// Check if it looks like a timestamp (starts with digit)
818: 		if len(name) > 0 && name[0] >= '0' && name[0] <= '9' {
819: 			legacyDirs = append(legacyDirs, name)
820: 		}
821: 	}
822: 
823: 	if len(legacyDirs) == 0 {
824: 		fmt.Println("✅ No legacy handoffs found - already using window-scoped structure")
825: 		return nil
826: 	}
827: 
828: 	// Show what will be migrated
829: 	fmt.Printf("Found %d legacy handoff(s) to migrate:\n\n", len(legacyDirs))
830: 	for _, dir := range legacyDirs {
831: 		handoffPath := filepath.Join(sessionBaseDir, dir, "SESSION_HANDOFF.md")
832: 		if _, err := os.Stat(handoffPath); err == nil {
833: 			fmt.Printf("  • %s → .orch/session/%s/%s\n", dir, windowName, dir)
834: 		}
835: 	}
836: 
837: 	fmt.Printf("\nMigrate to window-scoped structure for window %q? (y/N): ", windowName)
838: 	var response string
839: 	fmt.Scanln(&response)
840: 
841: 	if response != "y" && response != "Y" {
842: 		fmt.Println("Migration cancelled")
843: 		return nil
844: 	}
845: 
846: 	// Perform migration
847: 	windowScopedDir := filepath.Join(sessionBaseDir, windowName)
848: 	if err := os.MkdirAll(windowScopedDir, 0755); err != nil {
849: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to create window-scoped directory: %w", err)
850: 	}
851: 
852: 	migratedCount := 0
853: 	for _, dir := range legacyDirs {
854: 		sourcePath := filepath.Join(sessionBaseDir, dir)
855: 		destPath := filepath.Join(windowScopedDir, dir)
856: 
857: 		// Check if handoff exists
858: 		handoffPath := filepath.Join(sourcePath, "SESSION_HANDOFF.md")
859: 		if _, err := os.Stat(handoffPath); err != nil {
860: 			// Skip directories without handoffs
861: 			continue
862: 		}
863: 
864: 		// Move the directory

96: // BuildRelationshipGraph builds a graph of nodes connected by relationships
97: func BuildRelationshipGraph(nodes []*KnowledgeNode, relationships []Relationship) map[string][]*KnowledgeNode {
98: 	graph := make(map[string][]*KnowledgeNode)
99: 	nodeMap := make(map[string]*KnowledgeNode)
100: 
101: 	// Index nodes by their path AND by their ID (for models, ID is directory, Path is .md file)
102: 	for _, node := range nodes {
103: 		nodeMap[node.Path] = node
104: 		if node.ID != node.Path {
105: 			nodeMap[node.ID] = node
106: 		}
107: 	}
108: 
109: 	// Build parent-child relationships
110: 	for _, rel := range relationships {
111: 		parent := findNodeByPath(nodeMap, rel.From)
112: 		child := findNodeByPath(nodeMap, rel.To)
113: 
114: 		if parent != nil && child != nil {
115: 			// Add child to parent's children if not already there
116: 			found := false
117: 			for _, existingChild := range parent.Children {
118: 				if existingChild.ID == child.ID {
119: 					found = true
120: 					break
121: 				}
122: 			}
123: 			if !found {
124: 				parent.Children = append(parent.Children, child)
125: 			}
126: 
127: 			// Track in graph
128: 			graph[parent.ID] = append(graph[parent.ID], child)
129: 		}
130: 	}
131: 
132: 	return graph
133: }

135: // findNodeByPath finds a node by path, handling both exact matches and directory matches
136: func findNodeByPath(nodeMap map[string]*KnowledgeNode, path string) *KnowledgeNode {
137: 	// Try exact match first
138: 	if node, ok := nodeMap[path]; ok {
139: 		return node
140: 	}
141: 
142: 	// For directory paths (like .kb/models/completion-verification/), try to match by ID
143: 	// Models use directory as ID, so check if any node's ID matches this path
144: 	for _, node := range nodeMap {
145: 		if node.ID == path {
146: 			return node
147: 		}
148: 		// Also try matching directory to directory
149: 		if strings.HasSuffix(path, "/") && node.Type == NodeTypeModel {
150: 			if strings.HasSuffix(node.ID, "/") && node.ID == path {
151: 				return node
152: 			}
153: 			// Try without trailing slash
154: 			if node.ID == strings.TrimSuffix(path, "/") {
155: 				return node
156: 			}
157: 		}
158: 	}
159: 
160: 	return nil
161: }

172:

174: 		switch node.Type {

184: 	}

187: 	if investigationCount >= 15 && decisionCount == 0 && modelCount == 0 {
188: 		smells = append(smells, HealthSmell{
189: 			Type:        SmellNeedsSynthesis,
190: 			Description: "Many investigations without synthesis",
191: 			Count:       investigationCount,
192: 		})
193: 	}
194: 
195: 	// Smell 2: Model without probes = untested model claims
196: 	if modelCount > 0 && probeCount == 0 {
197: 		smells = append(smells, HealthSmell{
198: 			Type:        SmellUntestedModel,
199: 			Description: "Model without probe validation",
200: 			Count:       modelCount,
201: 		})
202: 	}
203: 
204: 	// Smell 3: Decision without spawned issues = not acted on
205: 	// This requires checking if any beads issues reference this decision
206: 	// For now, we'll skip this check as it requires beads integration
207: 	// TODO: Implement decision → issue tracking
208: 
209: 	return smells
210: }
